classroom.meaning: A table where a student sits to work
classroom.example: I keep my pencils and books inside my wooden desk.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A desk is where you sit and do your schoolwork.

classroom.meaning: A piece of furniture for sitting
classroom.example: My blue chair has four legs and a soft seat.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A chair is where you sit.

classroom.meaning: A shiny white board you write on with markers
classroom.example: The teacher writes math problems on the big whiteboard.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A whiteboard is where the teacher writes.

classroom.meaning: A dark green or black board you write on with chalk
classroom.example: The teacher uses white chalk on the chalkboard.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A chalkboard is a dark board you write on with chalk.

classroom.meaning: A piece of furniture that holds books
classroom.example: The bookshelf has many stories about animals and adventures.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A bookshelf holds all the classroom books.

classroom.example: We sit on the colorful carpet during story time.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A carpet is a soft rug on the floor.

classroom.meaning: An opening in the wall that lets in light and air
classroom.example: I look out the window and see birds in the tree.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A window is glass you can see through.

classroom.meaning: A movable barrier for entering or leaving a room
classroom.example: We line up by the door when it's time for recess.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A door is how you go in and out of a room.

classroom.meaning: A device that tells what time it is
classroom.example: The clock says 12 o'clock, so it's time for lunch.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A clock tells you the time.

classroom.meaning: A chart showing the days, weeks, and months
classroom.example: We look at the calendar to see which day is Friday.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A calendar shows the days of the week.

classroom.meaning: A small bag or box that holds writing tools
classroom.example: My pencil case has a zipper and holds ten pencils.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A pencil case keeps your pencils together.

classroom.meaning: A container for garbage and things you throw away
classroom.example: I throw my apple core in the trash can after snack.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A trash can holds things you throw away.

classroom.meaning: An electronic machine that can do many tasks
classroom.example: We use the computer to play learning games.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A computer is a machine that helps you learn.

classroom.meaning: A machine that shows pictures on a big screen
classroom.example: The teacher shows videos on the projector.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A projector makes pictures big on a screen.

classroom.meaning: A board where you pin papers and artwork
classroom.example: My drawing of a cat is on the bulletin board for everyone to see.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A bulletin board shows off your art.
